items.title: WA invokes emergency powers to address fuel supply crisis

children.content: The Western Australian government will use emergency powers to force fuel companies to provide information about their supply chains in an effort to keep fuel flowing through the state.

articles.title: Perth's 'Cheap fuel Tuesdays' are off the table. Here's why

children.content: Perth motorists can no longer rely on "cheap Tuesday" fuel prices and are urged to shop around every day as the Middle East war disrupts global supply chains.

image.altText: Cars lining up at a petrol station.

articles.title: PM says next months 'may not be easy', urges Australians to save fuel

children.content: Anthony Albanese asks Australians to trade their cars for public transport if they can, while reassuring those who need to drive they can continue to do so amid the global oil crisis.

image.altText: A man in a suit sits at a desk and reads a teleprompter, looking in to a camera.

articles.title: 'Ridiculous': How Australians are coping with sky-high fuel prices

children.content: The ABC asked how you were coping with fuel prices and here's what we heard back.

image.altText: A composite image of two men and two women.

Petrol pumps and prices at a service station.

items.title: WA man charged with terrorism offences wrote of Bondi attacks in diary, court told

children.content: Jayson Joseph Michaels, 20, had reflected on the Bondi attacks in his diary and was allegedly planning a "day of justice", a Perth court hears.

gallery.altText: A smiling youg man in high-vis in front of a digger holding a hose.

thumbnail.altText: A smiling youg man in high-vis in front of a digger holding a hose.

items.title: Western Australia at risk of mouse plague 'spiralling out of control'

children.content: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice which are making their way into residential areas.

image.altText: A metal mouse trap filled with mice.

items.title: Locals say country city of 40,000 sorely lacking public transport services

children.content: Busselton has just three regular bus services, leaving 85-year-old Jan Whalan with an hour's trip on her mobility scooter to get to the shops — but as the fuel crisis also bites, there are calls for better public transport.

image.altText: Portrait shot of Yalyalup resident Jan Whalan riding her mobility scooter.

items.title: A blood moon, a cyclone and rainbows — WA's skies, captured by you

children.content: Nature put on a special show for Western Australia in March, including a total lunar eclipse and rough seas from a powerful tropical cyclone, in addition to the spectacular sunsets the state is known for.

gallery.altText: A red moon is in the night sky
Rainbows are seen in the sky

thumbnail.altText: A red moon is in the night sky
Rainbows are seen in the sky

items.title: Perth house prices surge as Australia's two biggest cities go backwards

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A drone view of houses in a suburb of Perth.

items.title: WA young gun Connolly makes stunning IPL debut to seal thrilling win

children.content: Cooper Connolly puts in a game-winning performance for Punjab in his IPL debut.

image.altText: GettyImages-2252677170

items.title: Prized WA tourism hotspots forced to close to holiday-makers for Easter

children.content: Famed holiday hotspots in Western Australia's north, including Exmouth and Coral Bay, are reluctantly turning away tourists ahead of the Easter holiday peak as they rebuild in the wake of Cyclone Narelle.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a bright blue rugged coastline

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a bright blue rugged coastline

items.title: Damage to pastoral stations a 'kick in the guts' after cyclone

children.content: West Australian pastoralists have faced severe flooding after ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle wreaked havoc in one of the state's most important food-producing regions.

image.altText: An aerial view of flooded cattle station

items.title: 'Always assumed fuel would be available': Miner scales back as crisis bites

children.content: Junior West Australian iron ore miner Fenix Resources is forced to scale back non-essential activities to keep its operations running after coming within days of running out of fuel.

image.altText: Mining boss speaking at conference

items.title: 'No one deserves this': WA mum speaks out about vaccine decline

children.content: Naomi Steenson doesn't want to see other parents go through what she did with her son Blake, as new data shows 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their immunisations.

gallery.altText: A white woman with long black hair sitting at a kitchen table, looking at a baby photo
A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

thumbnail.altText: A white woman with long black hair sitting at a kitchen table, looking at a baby photo
A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

items.title: WA greyhound racing regulator defends controversial sport at inquiry

children.content: The body governing greyhound racing in WA says it is determined to maintain the welfare of both the dogs and the industry at a parliamentary inquiry, which was sparked by a petition that garnered 26,000 signatures.

image.altText: Greyhounds racing.

items.title: Man accused of failed Invasion Day rally bomb may enter insanity plea

children.content: A man accused of tossing a homemade "fragment bomb" filled with ball bearings and screws into a crowd in Perth's CBD flags through his lawyer that he may argue he is not criminally responsible for his actions due to insanity.

image.altText: Police speak to a man wearing a black butterfly print T-shirt, baseball cap and face mask

items.title: Man jailed for life for fatal stabbing of 'kind and loving' woman

children.content: Samuel Jade Lincoln Jingle will have to serve a minimum of 19 years in prison after killing his partner in Fitzroy Crossing.

image.altText: Exterior shot of the WA Supreme Court building in Perth with red kangaroo paws in the foreground at left.

items.title: Black-robed lawyers and 'killer' beef at Kimberley court

children.content: It was a court hearing unlike any other, complete with country music, "killer" beef, and gaggles of kids and dogs dashing between the black-clad lawyers.

image.altText: A man with a guitar surrounded by children

items.title: How one man's stolen identity was used to run a cult

children.content: In mid-2009 British citizen Simon Kadwill-Kelly was catching a ferry back from France. But as he tried to board, he learned his identity had been stolen — by the man accused of running an international cult from Nannup.

items.title: How this Aussie went from TAFE to the head of Sony character animation

children.content: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

items.title: Travel quiz: Get out your passport and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which is Australia’s oldest Luna Park? And where would you find Elvis Presley’s mansion?

image.altText: Sydney Luna Park entrance

items.title: Bluey's lead composer on helping kids fall in love with music

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

articles.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss

children.content: Italy's World Cup exile continues after the four-time champions fall to a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat by Bosnia and Herzegovina.

image.altText: Pio Esposito reacts after missing a penalty against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

articles.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies

children.content: Nestory Irankunda scores twice as the Socceroos' substitutes spark a 5-1 win over Curaçao in their FIFA Series clash in Melbourne.

image.altText: Irankunda backflip

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: Cocoa prices crash but Easter chocolate stays expensive

children.content: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

image.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

items.title: Transphobia nearly killed Kaz. Now he just wants to be 'me'

children.content: Growing up in Bunbury was pretty standard for Kaz — until puberty, when the WA teenager became depressed, ill and ended up missing out on school. He says it was part of the toll transphobia took on him.

gallery.altText: A boy stands in front of ocean scenery.
